114	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:159:| Kit / Apparatus | Rig (plugin package) | Kit / Apparatus |
115	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:160:| The Books | nexus.db / SQLite tables | The Stacks (`books` apparatus) |
116	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:161:| Summon relay | built-in clockworks dispatch | summon relay (installed via nexus-stdlib) |
117	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:162:| Arbor | Arbor | Arbor |
118	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:163:| Spider | (not yet implemented) | The Spider (`spider` apparatus) |
119	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:164:| Fabricator | (not yet implemented) | The Fabricator (`fabricator` apparatus) |
120	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:166:---
121	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:168:## Session Notes
122	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:170:- **2026-03-31 (session 1):** Initial scaffold session. Wrote §1–4 scaffold + "Standard Guild" bridge section. Created this context doc. Architecture doc is at `docs/architecture/index.md`. Companion detailed docs are already written for clockworks, plugins, kit-components, and rigging — they're good references even if partially aspirational.
123	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:172:[Omitted long matching line]
124	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:174:[Omitted long matching line]
125	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:176:---
126	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:178:## Design Decisions (session 3)
127	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:180:### Plugin name derivation
128	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:182:Plugin ids are derived from npm package names with three rules applied in order:
129	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:183:1. Strip `@shardworks/` scope entirely (bare name)
130	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:184:2. Retain other scopes as prefix without `@` (`@acme/foo` → `acme/foo`)
131	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:185:3. Strip trailing `-(plugin|apparatus|kit)` suffix
132	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:187:This means `@shardworks/clockworks` → `clockworks`, `@shardworks/books-apparatus` → `books`, `@acme/cache-apparatus` → `acme/cache`. Documented in `plugins.md` (Plugin IDs section). **Not yet implemented** — see implementation plan.
133	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:189:### Plugin configuration access
134	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:191:Config sections live at the top level of `guild.json` under the plugin's derived id. Because `@shardworks/clockworks` → `clockworks`, the Clockworks apparatus gets `guild.json["clockworks"]` naturally — no privileged handling.
135	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:193:Access is via `guild().config<T>(pluginId)` — always requires an explicit plugin id (no implicit scoping). `guild().guildConfig()` is the escape hatch for framework-level fields.
136	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:195:Documented in `plugins.md` (Plugin IDs section + Configuration section). **Implemented** in session 4.
137	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:197:### guild() singleton — replaces HandlerContext
138	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:199:**Problem identified:** `HandlerContext` was injected into tool handlers as a second parameter, but the MCP server created a broken stub (all methods threw), and the pattern required a context factory in Arbor, the CLI, and the CDC registry.
139	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:201:**Decision:** Replace with a process-level singleton `guild()` from `@shardworks/nexus-core`. All plugin code — apparatus `start()`, tool handlers, CDC handlers — calls `guild()` to access `home`, `apparatus()`, `config()`, `guildConfig()`, `kits()`, `apparatuses()`.
140	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:203:Arbor creates the `Guild` instance before starting any apparatus (backed by the live `provides` Map, so dependency ordering works). `setGuild()` and `clearGuild()` are exported for testing.
141	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:205:`HandlerContext` and `GuildContext` removed from plugin.ts. `createHandlerContext` removed from Arbor interface. `createMinimalHandlerContext` removed from CLI. Tool handler signature: `(params) => unknown | Promise<unknown>` — no context parameter.
142	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:207:### GuildContext → StartupContext
143	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:209:**Problem:** `GuildContext` (passed to apparatus `start()`) overlapped with `guild()` — same methods (`apparatus()`, `config()`, `home`, etc.), different scoping behavior. Two contexts with similar methods but different semantics is confusing.
144	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:211:**Decision:** Strip `GuildContext` down to `StartupContext` with a single method: `on(event, handler)` for lifecycle event subscription. All other guild access in `start()` goes through `guild()`, same as everywhere else. No overlap, no confusion.
145	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:213:### GuildConfigV2 → GuildConfig
146	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:215:Renamed everywhere. Dropped V2 suffixes from `createInitialGuildConfig`, `readGuildConfig`, `writeGuildConfig`. Legacy V1 `GuildConfig` untouched in its own module scope (`legacy/1/guild-config.ts`).
147	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:217:### CDC handlers — no context injection
148	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:219:CDC handlers (`ChangeHandler`) no longer receive a context parameter. They capture dependencies via closure from the `start()` scope where they're registered. Signature: `(event: ChangeEvent<T>) => Promise<void> | void`.
149	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:221:---
150	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:223:## Next Steps for Architecture Doc (`index.md`)
151	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:225:### Completed sections
152	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:226:- **§1 Introduction** ✅
153	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:227:- **§2 System at a Glance** ✅ — scoped as standard guild, ASCII diagram, narrative subsections
154	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:228:- **§3 The Guild Root** ✅ — directory structure, guild.json (framework keys + plugin config), .nexus/ runtime state
155	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:229:- **§4 Plugin Architecture** ✅ — §2 callback, Kit/Apparatus examples, Plugin IDs, guild() singleton, StartupContext, Installation
156	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:230:- **The Standard Guild** ✅ — apparatus table (plugin ids) and kit table
157	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:231:- **The Books** ✅ — Stacks apparatus, document model, API surface, CDC, backend
158	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:232:- **Kit Components** ✅ — tools/engines/relays, comparison table, link to kit-components.md
159	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:234:### Remaining stub sections
160	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:235:All are `<!-- TODO -->` blocks. In rough priority order:
161	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:237:1. **Work Model** — Commission → Mandate writ → child writs → Rigs. Writ lifecycle states (`ready → active → pending → completed/failed/cancelled`). Writ hierarchy and completion rollup. Brief rig intro (Spider assembles from engine designs via Fabricator). Link to `rigging.md`.
162	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:239:2. **The Clockworks** — Abbreviate; `clockworks.md` is detailed and current. Cover: events as immutable facts, standing orders as guild policy, summon verb, framework vs custom events, runner (manual vs daemon), error handling. Link to `clockworks.md`.
163	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:241:3. **Animas** — MVP: no identity layer. Composition is per-role, not per-anima. The Loom weaves caller-provided system prompt into a session context (pass-through for MVP). Future: anima identity records, curricula, temperaments, states (active/retired). Keep section light on implementation since apparatus are being designed.
164	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:243:4. **Sessions** — Session funnel. Triggered by summon relay or `nsg consult`. Loom → Animator → AI process → result recorded. Session providers (pluggable). System prompt vs initial prompt. Bare mode. Link to `reference/conversations.md`.
165	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:245:5. **Core Apparatus Reference** — Quick-reference table with plugin ids, package names, API surface hints, links to detailed docs.
166	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:247:### Implementation work (not architecture doc)
167	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:248:- **guild() singleton** ✅ — implemented in session 4. `Guild` interface, `setGuild`/`clearGuild`, Arbor wiring, all handlers migrated.
168	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:249:- **GuildContext → StartupContext** ✅ — implemented in session 4. HandlerContext removed. createHandlerContext removed from Arbor.
169	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:250:- **GuildConfigV2 → GuildConfig** ✅ — renamed everywhere in session 4.
170	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:251:- **Plugin rename** — standard apparatus packages should be renamed to match new naming convention (e.g. `@shardworks/nexus-clockworks` → `@shardworks/clockworks`). Not yet commissioned. Scope TBD.
171	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:252:- **The Instrumentarium** — specs at `apparatus/instrumentarium.md`. Not yet implemented.
172	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:253:- **Loom MVP** — specs at `apparatus/loom.md`. Not yet implemented.
173	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:254:- **Animator MVP** — specs at `apparatus/animator.md`. Not yet implemented.
174	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:256:---
175	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:258:## Design Decisions (session 4)
176	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:260:### New apparatus: The Instrumentarium (`tools`)
177	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:262:**Problem:** Tools are currently owned by Arbor (`listTools()`, `findTool()`), but Arbor's design goal is "plugin loader only." Tools need a home that both the session layer (Loom/Animator) and the CLI can depend on, without coupling either to anima identity.
178	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:264:**Decision:** Create a new apparatus — **The Instrumentarium** (plugin id `tools`, package `@shardworks/tools-apparatus`). It owns:
179	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:265:- Tool registry — scanning kit `tools` contributions and apparatus `supportKit` tools at startup
180	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:266:- Role-gating resolution — given a set of roles + baseTools, return the resolved tool set
181	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:267:- CLI tool discovery — `nsg <tool>` resolves through The Instrumentarium
182	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:269:The Instrumentarium has no dependency on animas, sessions, or composition. Both The Loom and the CLI depend on it independently. Apparatus that need to invoke tools programmatically depend on it.
183	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:271:`consumes: ["tools"]` — scans kit and supportKit contributions for tool definitions.
184	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:273:### Loom MVP — composition without identity
185	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:275:**Problem:** Full anima composition (identity lookup → curriculum resolution → temperament resolution → charter + tool instructions) requires several systems that don't exist yet. But The Animator needs *some* composed context to launch sessions.
186	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:277:**Decision:** MVP Loom is a pass-through — the caller provides the system prompt and optional initial prompt. The Loom packages them into a `WovenContext` that The Animator consumes. No role resolution, no tool instructions, no file reading, no identity lookup.
187	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:279:The Loom exists as a separate apparatus even at MVP so that The Animator never assembles prompts itself. As composition grows (role instructions, tool instructions, curricula, temperaments, charter), The Loom's internals change but its output shape (`WovenContext`) stays stable — The Animator is unaffected.
188	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:281:### Animator MVP
189	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:283:**Decision:** MVP Animator takes a `WovenContext` (from Loom) + a working directory and:
190	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:284:1. Launches a session provider (e.g. `claude-code-apparatus`) with the system prompt
191	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:285:2. Monitors the process
192	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:286:3. Records the session result to The Stacks (sessions book)
193	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:288:No MCP tool server, no Instrumentarium dependency, no role awareness in MVP. Tool-equipped sessions with MCP are documented as future state in `apparatus/animator.md`.
194	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:290:### Dependency graph (MVP)
195	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:292:```
196	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:293:The Stacks (books)
197	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:294:    │
198	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:295:    └── The Animator (animator)
199	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:296:            │
200	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:297:            └── The Loom (loom)   ← zero apparatus dependencies, pass-through
201	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:299:The Clockworks (clockworks)
202	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:300:    │
203	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:301:    └── summon relay → The Loom → The Animator
204	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:303:The Instrumentarium (tools)   ← no dependencies in MVP, not yet wired to sessions
205	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:304:    │
206	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:305:    └── CLI (nsg)
207	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:306:```
208	docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:308:Note: in MVP, The Loom and The Animator do not depend on The Instrumentarium. Tool-equipped sessions (Animator → Instrumentarium for MCP tool set) are future state.

docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:1:# Agent Context: Architecture Doc Codebase Scan
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:3:> **Purpose:** Notes for agents working on `docs/architecture/index.md` so they don't have to re-scan the codebase from scratch. Written during the initial scaffolding session (2026-03-31). May drift from reality — treat as orientation, not ground truth.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:5:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:7:## Repo Layout
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:9:The Nexus framework lives at `/workspace/nexus/`. Key directories:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:11:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:12:/workspace/nexus/
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:13:  packages/               ← TypeScript packages (pnpm workspace)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:14:  docs/
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:15:    architecture/         ← THIS IS WHERE YOU ARE
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:16:    reference/            ← API reference (core-api.md, schema.md, event-catalog.md, conversations.md)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:17:    guides/               ← How-to guides (building-engines.md, building-tools.md)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:18:    guild-metaphor.md     ← Conceptual vocabulary; read this first
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:19:    philosophy.md         ← Project "why"
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:20:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:22:The live guild workspace (where animas operate) is at `/workspace/shardworks/`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:24:The patron-side sanctum (experiments, session notes, Coco config) is at `/workspace/nexus-mk2/`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:26:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:28:## Packages
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:30:| Package | npm name | What it is |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:31:|---------|----------|------------|
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:32:| `core` | `@shardworks/nexus-core` | Shared library — Books, config, path utilities, writ/anima/event functions, `tool()` and `engine()` SDK factories, `Rig` type |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:33:| `arbor` | `@shardworks/nexus-arbor` (approx) | Guild runtime object — loads plugins (currently "rigs"), manages tool registry, owns Books database connection |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:34:| `cli` | `@shardworks/nexus` | The `nsg` CLI binary |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:35:| `nexus-clockworks` | `@shardworks/nexus-clockworks` | Clockworks as a rig — contributes clockworks tools and events/dispatches Books tables |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:36:| `nexus-sessions` | `@shardworks/nexus-sessions` | Sessions as a rig — contributes session tools and sessions Book |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:37:| `guild-starter-kit` | `@shardworks/guild-starter-kit` | Starter bundle — curricula, temperaments, migration snapshots |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:38:| `claude-code-apparatus` | `@shardworks/claude-code-apparatus` | Session provider implementation for Claude Code / claude CLI |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:39:| `stdlib` | `@shardworks/nexus-stdlib` | Standard tools, engines, relays |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:41:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:43:## The Rig Terminology Collision
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:45:**This is the most important thing to understand before touching this doc.**
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:47:The word "rig" means two completely different things in this codebase:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:49:| Context | Meaning |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:50:|---------|---------|
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:51:| **Guild metaphor / target architecture** | The execution scaffold assembled to fulfill a commission — seeded at commission time, built out by Spider with engines, struck when work is done |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:52:| **Current code** (`Rig` type in `core/src/rig.ts`, loaded by Arbor) | A package contributing tools, Books declarations, and other capabilities to the guild — basically what the target architecture calls a Kit or Apparatus |
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:54:The current code's `Rig` is what we're moving toward calling a **Kit** (or Apparatus, for packages with a lifecycle). This rename is in progress. When reading source code, mentally substitute "plugin" for `Rig`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:56:The architecture docs use "rig" exclusively in the metaphor sense (execution scaffold). The source code uses it in the plugin sense. Both are in the same repo. Don't mix them up.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:58:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:60:## Architecture Docs Status
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:62:### Exists and reasonably current
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:64:| Doc | Status | Notes |

docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:142:## guild.json Shape
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:144:The V2 type (`GuildConfig` in `packages/core/src/guild-config.ts`) defines the framework keys. All other top-level keys are plugin configuration sections, keyed by derived plugin id.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:146:**Framework keys:** `name`, `nexus`, `plugins` (string array), `settings` (object with `model`, `autoMigrate`).
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:148:**Plugin config keys (standard guild):** `clockworks`, `codexes`, `roles`, `baseTools` — owned by their respective apparatus, not by the framework. They sit at the top level because `@shardworks/clockworks` → `clockworks`, `@shardworks/codexes-apparatus` → `codexes`, etc.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:150:Note: the live guild at `/workspace/shardworks/` is still running the V1 config shape (per-capability registries: `tools`, `engines`, `curricula`, `temperaments` as objects, no `plugins` array). V2 has `plugins` as a flat string array and drops per-capability registries. The architecture docs describe V2.

docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:178:## Design Decisions (session 3)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:180:### Plugin name derivation
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:182:Plugin ids are derived from npm package names with three rules applied in order:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:183:1. Strip `@shardworks/` scope entirely (bare name)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:184:2. Retain other scopes as prefix without `@` (`@acme/foo` → `acme/foo`)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:185:3. Strip trailing `-(plugin|apparatus|kit)` suffix
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:187:This means `@shardworks/clockworks` → `clockworks`, `@shardworks/books-apparatus` → `books`, `@acme/cache-apparatus` → `acme/cache`. Documented in `plugins.md` (Plugin IDs section). **Not yet implemented** — see implementation plan.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:189:### Plugin configuration access
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:191:Config sections live at the top level of `guild.json` under the plugin's derived id. Because `@shardworks/clockworks` → `clockworks`, the Clockworks apparatus gets `guild.json["clockworks"]` naturally — no privileged handling.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:193:Access is via `guild().config<T>(pluginId)` — always requires an explicit plugin id (no implicit scoping). `guild().guildConfig()` is the escape hatch for framework-level fields.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:195:Documented in `plugins.md` (Plugin IDs section + Configuration section). **Implemented** in session 4.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:197:### guild() singleton — replaces HandlerContext
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:199:**Problem identified:** `HandlerContext` was injected into tool handlers as a second parameter, but the MCP server created a broken stub (all methods threw), and the pattern required a context factory in Arbor, the CLI, and the CDC registry.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:201:**Decision:** Replace with a process-level singleton `guild()` from `@shardworks/nexus-core`. All plugin code — apparatus `start()`, tool handlers, CDC handlers — calls `guild()` to access `home`, `apparatus()`, `config()`, `guildConfig()`, `kits()`, `apparatuses()`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:203:Arbor creates the `Guild` instance before starting any apparatus (backed by the live `provides` Map, so dependency ordering works). `setGuild()` and `clearGuild()` are exported for testing.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:205:`HandlerContext` and `GuildContext` removed from plugin.ts. `createHandlerContext` removed from Arbor interface. `createMinimalHandlerContext` removed from CLI. Tool handler signature: `(params) => unknown | Promise<unknown>` — no context parameter.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:207:### GuildContext → StartupContext
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:209:**Problem:** `GuildContext` (passed to apparatus `start()`) overlapped with `guild()` — same methods (`apparatus()`, `config()`, `home`, etc.), different scoping behavior. Two contexts with similar methods but different semantics is confusing.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:211:**Decision:** Strip `GuildContext` down to `StartupContext` with a single method: `on(event, handler)` for lifecycle event subscription. All other guild access in `start()` goes through `guild()`, same as everywhere else. No overlap, no confusion.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:213:### GuildConfigV2 → GuildConfig
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:215:Renamed everywhere. Dropped V2 suffixes from `createInitialGuildConfig`, `readGuildConfig`, `writeGuildConfig`. Legacy V1 `GuildConfig` untouched in its own module scope (`legacy/1/guild-config.ts`).
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:217:### CDC handlers — no context injection
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:219:CDC handlers (`ChangeHandler`) no longer receive a context parameter. They capture dependencies via closure from the `start()` scope where they're registered. Signature: `(event: ChangeEvent<T>) => Promise<void> | void`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:221:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:223:## Next Steps for Architecture Doc (`index.md`)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:225:### Completed sections
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:226:- **§1 Introduction** ✅
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:227:- **§2 System at a Glance** ✅ — scoped as standard guild, ASCII diagram, narrative subsections
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:228:- **§3 The Guild Root** ✅ — directory structure, guild.json (framework keys + plugin config), .nexus/ runtime state
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:229:- **§4 Plugin Architecture** ✅ — §2 callback, Kit/Apparatus examples, Plugin IDs, guild() singleton, StartupContext, Installation
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:230:- **The Standard Guild** ✅ — apparatus table (plugin ids) and kit table
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:231:- **The Books** ✅ — Stacks apparatus, document model, API surface, CDC, backend
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:232:- **Kit Components** ✅ — tools/engines/relays, comparison table, link to kit-components.md
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:234:### Remaining stub sections
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:235:All are `<!-- TODO -->` blocks. In rough priority order:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:237:1. **Work Model** — Commission → Mandate writ → child writs → Rigs. Writ lifecycle states (`ready → active → pending → completed/failed/cancelled`). Writ hierarchy and completion rollup. Brief rig intro (Spider assembles from engine designs via Fabricator). Link to `rigging.md`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:239:2. **The Clockworks** — Abbreviate; `clockworks.md` is detailed and current. Cover: events as immutable facts, standing orders as guild policy, summon verb, framework vs custom events, runner (manual vs daemon), error handling. Link to `clockworks.md`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:241:3. **Animas** — MVP: no identity layer. Composition is per-role, not per-anima. The Loom weaves caller-provided system prompt into a session context (pass-through for MVP). Future: anima identity records, curricula, temperaments, states (active/retired). Keep section light on implementation since apparatus are being designed.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:243:4. **Sessions** — Session funnel. Triggered by summon relay or `nsg consult`. Loom → Animator → AI process → result recorded. Session providers (pluggable). System prompt vs initial prompt. Bare mode. Link to `reference/conversations.md`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:245:5. **Core Apparatus Reference** — Quick-reference table with plugin ids, package names, API surface hints, links to detailed docs.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:247:### Implementation work (not architecture doc)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:248:- **guild() singleton** ✅ — implemented in session 4. `Guild` interface, `setGuild`/`clearGuild`, Arbor wiring, all handlers migrated.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:249:- **GuildContext → StartupContext** ✅ — implemented in session 4. HandlerContext removed. createHandlerContext removed from Arbor.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:250:- **GuildConfigV2 → GuildConfig** ✅ — renamed everywhere in session 4.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:251:- **Plugin rename** — standard apparatus packages should be renamed to match new naming convention (e.g. `@shardworks/nexus-clockworks` → `@shardworks/clockworks`). Not yet commissioned. Scope TBD.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:252:- **The Instrumentarium** — specs at `apparatus/instrumentarium.md`. Not yet implemented.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:253:- **Loom MVP** — specs at `apparatus/loom.md`. Not yet implemented.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:254:- **Animator MVP** — specs at `apparatus/animator.md`. Not yet implemented.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:256:---
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:258:## Design Decisions (session 4)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:260:### New apparatus: The Instrumentarium (`tools`)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:262:**Problem:** Tools are currently owned by Arbor (`listTools()`, `findTool()`), but Arbor's design goal is "plugin loader only." Tools need a home that both the session layer (Loom/Animator) and the CLI can depend on, without coupling either to anima identity.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:264:**Decision:** Create a new apparatus — **The Instrumentarium** (plugin id `tools`, package `@shardworks/tools-apparatus`). It owns:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:265:- Tool registry — scanning kit `tools` contributions and apparatus `supportKit` tools at startup
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:266:- Role-gating resolution — given a set of roles + baseTools, return the resolved tool set
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:267:- CLI tool discovery — `nsg <tool>` resolves through The Instrumentarium
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:269:The Instrumentarium has no dependency on animas, sessions, or composition. Both The Loom and the CLI depend on it independently. Apparatus that need to invoke tools programmatically depend on it.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:271:`consumes: ["tools"]` — scans kit and supportKit contributions for tool definitions.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:273:### Loom MVP — composition without identity
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:275:**Problem:** Full anima composition (identity lookup → curriculum resolution → temperament resolution → charter + tool instructions) requires several systems that don't exist yet. But The Animator needs *some* composed context to launch sessions.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:277:**Decision:** MVP Loom is a pass-through — the caller provides the system prompt and optional initial prompt. The Loom packages them into a `WovenContext` that The Animator consumes. No role resolution, no tool instructions, no file reading, no identity lookup.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:279:The Loom exists as a separate apparatus even at MVP so that The Animator never assembles prompts itself. As composition grows (role instructions, tool instructions, curricula, temperaments, charter), The Loom's internals change but its output shape (`WovenContext`) stays stable — The Animator is unaffected.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:281:### Animator MVP
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:283:**Decision:** MVP Animator takes a `WovenContext` (from Loom) + a working directory and:
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:284:1. Launches a session provider (e.g. `claude-code-apparatus`) with the system prompt
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:285:2. Monitors the process
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:286:3. Records the session result to The Stacks (sessions book)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:288:No MCP tool server, no Instrumentarium dependency, no role awareness in MVP. Tool-equipped sessions with MCP are documented as future state in `apparatus/animator.md`.
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:290:### Dependency graph (MVP)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:292:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:293:The Stacks (books)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:294:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:295:    └── The Animator (animator)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:296:            │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:297:            └── The Loom (loom)   ← zero apparatus dependencies, pass-through
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:299:The Clockworks (clockworks)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:300:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:301:    └── summon relay → The Loom → The Animator
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:303:The Instrumentarium (tools)   ← no dependencies in MVP, not yet wired to sessions
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:304:    │
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:305:    └── CLI (nsg)
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:306:```
docs/architecture/_agent-context.md:308:Note: in MVP, The Loom and The Animator do not depend on The Instrumentarium. Tool-equipped sessions (Animator → Instrumentarium for MCP tool set) are future state.
